preferred_translation: with ignorant wanting as condition, taking personally
definition: Forward-link line connecting taṇhā and upādāna.
notes: Use this record for the whole line. It keeps the repository's house handling of taṇhā and upādāna together at the key transition from wanting into appropriation. The OSF glossary supports this coordinated inheritance from taṇhā and upādāna in the formula line.

context_rules.rendering: with ignorant wanting as condition, taking personally

context_rules.context: When a controlled doctrinal alternate for upādāna is already in use
context_rules.rendering: with ignorant wanting as condition, clinging
context_rules.notes: Use only when the surrounding frame explicitly requires it.

example_phrases.translation: with ignorant wanting as condition, taking personally

authority_basis.scope: Supports coordinated inheritance from taṇhā and upādāna in the formula line.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard line linking taṇhā and upādāna in the forward chain.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not replace the upādāna side with generic attachment language unless a controlled alternate is already governing the passage.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift at the chain's pivot from wanting into appropriation.
